summary: Hospital Appointment System on the Internet Computer with a Motoko backend canister providing doctor directory, patient profile storage, and appointment booking/rescheduling/cancellation, plus an AI chatbot that uses an external HTTP outcall with a robust local symptom-guidance fallback. The frontend is a simplified, mobile-friendly React SPA with a splash/loading screen, improved doctors loading/empty states, and a basic form-based sign-in/profile experience that uses a stable pseudo-identity for per-user data separation without relying on Internet Identity.

existing_features.title: Internet Identity authentication context
existing_features.description: Provides an InternetIdentityProvider and useInternetIdentity hook that initializes AuthClient, loads persisted sessions, supports login via Internet Identity, exposes login status flags, and supports logout/clear operations.

existing_features.title: Backend actor creation per identity with access-control initialization
existing_features.description: Creates a backend actor with anonymous or authenticated identity, initializes backend access control by calling _initializeAccessControlWithSecret using a secret token from URL hash/session, and invalidates/refetches dependent React Query queries when the actor changes.

existing_features.title: Role-based access control (admin/user/guest)
existing_features.description: Motoko AccessControl module and MixinAuthorization provide role detection, admin checks, and admin-only role assignment. Initialization uses an environment-provided admin token and a user-provided secret to assign the first matching principal as admin.

existing_features.title: Patient profile management (create/update/save/get)
existing_features.description: Backend supports saving/creating/updating a caller’s patient profile and querying profiles (optional and trapping variants). Frontend provides React Query hooks and a Profile page UI to create/edit profile data with basic validation and success/error toasts.

existing_features.title: Doctor directory with seed data, details, and availability
existing_features.description: Backend maintains a catalog of doctor profiles (name, specialization, years, hospital, slots) and exposes getAllDoctors, getDoctorById, and getDoctorAvailability that filters out already-booked slots. Frontend fetches and displays the directory.

existing_features.title: Backend initialization hook for seeding doctors
existing_features.description: Frontend useInitializeBackend triggers backend system_install_was() once per session when an actor becomes available, ensuring doctor seed data exists for browsing immediately after deployment.

existing_features.title: Appointment booking with slot conflict prevention
existing_features.description: Backend allows callers to book appointments for doctor time slots while preventing double-booking of the same slot for active (non-cancelled) appointments. Frontend BookingModal lets users pick an available slot and submit the booking with loading state and toasts.

existing_features.title: Appointment listing and management (reschedule/cancel)
existing_features.description: Backend exposes getPatientAppointments and supports rescheduling/cancelling appointments with ownership checks and status transitions (booked/rescheduled/cancelled). Frontend Appointments page shows upcoming/history tabs and provides a modal to reschedule or cancel using available slots.

existing_features.title: Medical chatbot integration via HTTP outcalls
existing_features.description: Backend performs an HTTP GET outcall to an external medical-chatbot service using IC.http_request with a transform function. Frontend provides a ChatbotWidget UI for interactive Q&A with pending state and graceful fallback messaging.

existing_features.title: Admin-only maintenance placeholders
existing_features.description: Backend includes an admin-only runScheduledNotifications entry point as a placeholder for future notification logic; access is enforced via AccessControl.isAdmin.

existing_features.title: Single-page UI shell with bottom navigation
existing_features.description: Frontend App maintains a simple in-memory page state (home/doctors/appointments/profile) and renders corresponding pages; BottomNav provides fixed navigation and also supports a custom window 'navigate' event listener.

existing_features.title: HTTP outcall utility module with response transform
existing_features.description: Reusable Motoko module wrapping IC.http_request for GET and POST with fixed cycle budget, request headers, and UTF-8 body decoding; includes a transform function that strips response headers for certified variables/compatibility.

existing_features.title: Upgrade migration scaffold
existing_features.description: Includes a migration module and uses `(with migration = Migration.run)` to enable upgrade migration flows; current migration is a no-op that preserves prior state shape for future evolution.

existing_features.title: Splash/loading screen on app launch
existing_features.description: Frontend displays a lightweight splash/loading screen (project name/branding) during initial app boot to avoid a blank white screen before the main UI renders.

existing_features.title: Chatbot robust response: URL-encoded outcall + local symptom-guidance fallback
existing_features.description: Backend askMedicalChatbot now URL-encodes the user question for the external HTTP outcall and guarantees a helpful response by falling back to local symptom-intent detection (covering common symptoms) with safe home-remedy tips and a recommended doctor selected from the in-canister doctor directory via symptom→specialization mapping.

existing_features.title: Automatic and idempotent doctor seeding
existing_features.description: Backend ensures doctor seed data exists automatically and idempotently so the doctor list is consistently available on app open across environments, without requiring a one-time client initialization call and without creating duplicates across repeated calls/upgrades.

existing_features.title: Improved Doctors page loading/empty-state behavior
existing_features.description: Frontend Doctors page shows a loading state while the doctor list query is in-flight and only shows a true empty-state when the backend returns an empty list, preventing misleading "No doctors found" during initial load/refresh.

existing_features.title: Form-based auth with stable pseudo-identity for per-user data separation
existing_features.description: Frontend uses a simple form-based sign-in/profile flow and a stable pseudo-identity (persisted client-side) to separate user data without requiring Internet Identity; actor creation uses this pseudo-identity to scope profile/appointments per user.

existing_features.title: Chatbot UI readability and actionable error handling
existing_features.description: Frontend chatbot preserves multi-line formatting for responses and displays an actionable error message when a chatbot request fails, while keeping chat state stable and readable.

existing_features.title: Ensure the frontend chatbot UI cleanly displays the updated guidance + doctor recommendation text returned by the backend (multi-paragraph, readable formatting), without showing raw/debug output.

new_feature_requests.summary: Update AI chatbot to provide simple symptom-based next-step guidance (non-diagnostic) and recommend an appropriate doctor from the existing doctor list (symptom→specialization mapping) with example remedies/precautions

Update the backend chatbot behavior so that when a user asks about symptoms (e.g., fever, headache, cold), the system returns a simple, non-diagnostic response that (1) acknowledges the symptoms, (2) provides safe next-step guidance (self-care + monitoring), (3) lists clear red-flag symptoms that require urgent care, and (4) recommends an appropriate doctor specialization based on a symptom→specialization mapping.

Enhance the backend chatbot response to recommend doctors from the existing seeded doctor directory by including at least one matching doctor name (and their specialization) when the recommended specialization exists in the current doctor list.

Make the backend chatbot HTTP outcall construction safe for user-entered text by URL-encoding the user question before building the request URL, to prevent broken requests for spaces/special characters.

architecturalNotes: Internet Computer architecture: Motoko actor canister backend; React SPA frontend consuming the canister via generated actor interface.
Frontend data layer built on @tanstack/react-query with query keys, cache invalidation, and mutations for all backend operations; toast notifications via sonner.
Authentication/identity: Internet Identity AuthClient provider supplies an Identity; actor instances are created per-principal and re-created on identity change.

Backend authorization implemented as a mixin (MixinAuthorization) wrapping a reusable AccessControl state module; first valid-token caller becomes admin, others become user; anonymous treated as guest.

Backend persistence modeled with in-memory Map structures keyed by Principal (patients) and Nat (doctors/appointments); doctor seed data is created by an explicit initialization function and a publicly callable install function.
Backend integrates external HTTP outcalls via the management canister (IC.http_request) with a transform function that strips response headers.
Upgrade/migration scaffold present via `(with migration = Migration.run)` and a migration.mo module (currently a no-op), indicating intent to preserve state across upgrades.
UI is a simple in-app page switcher (no router) with a fixed bottom navigation bar; modals are used for booking and managing appointments. (UI components appear to be shadcn/radix-style primitives).

A reusable build-template was added/updated for Internet Computer projects (Motoko backend + React frontend), including canister.yaml templates, deploy scripts, workspace package management, and frontend tooling (ESLint custom rules, shadcn/ui component set, Vite/Tailwind config).

Auth direction change: move away from Internet Identity login toward a simple form-based sign-in/profile flow (name/phone/email) with logout and intended per-user data separation; requires corresponding backend identity/authorization approach (no longer principal-based via II).

Frontend authentication now supports a form-based flow using a client-side persisted pseudo-identity (pseudoPrincipal) to achieve per-user data separation without relying on Internet Identity principals.
Doctor seed initialization is performed automatically and idempotently on the backend, removing reliance on a client-triggered initialization hook for production correctness.
Chatbot backend guarantees non-empty responses by combining URL-safe external outcalls with a deterministic local symptom-guidance fallback sourced from in-canister doctor data.
knownIssues: Backend comments and logic allow anonymous callers to create profiles and book/manage appointments; on the IC, the anonymous principal is shared across all unauthenticated users, which can cause all guest users to overwrite each other’s profiles and see/manage the same appointment set.

Doctor seeding is invoked via system_install_was() and also in postupgrade(); initializeDoctors() does not clear existing data and increments nextDoctorId, so calling system_install_was multiple times can create duplicate doctors with new IDs over time (especially if frontend triggers it after upgrades or multiple sessions).

Frontend BookingModal displays profile-required/Unauthorized flows, but backend bookAppointment() does not check profile completeness and does not return an Unauthorized error in that case—UI logic likely won’t behave as intended.
Time slot conflict detection uses (day + startTime) match and ignores endTime; no validation is performed on time formats or overlapping time ranges.

No stable variables are explicitly marked in main.mo; state persistence relies on the migration mechanism pattern, but without seeing stable declarations it’s unclear whether doctor/patient/appointment maps persist as expected across upgrades.
React Query actor-change effect invalidates and refetches all non-actor queries, which may cause a burst of refetches and duplicated backend initialization attempts.

Form-based auth/pseudo-identity is not cryptographically secure and should not be treated as strong authentication/authorization; it is suitable only for basic per-device/per-browser separation unless replaced with a real identity provider.
AI chatbot behavior is not matching intended simple symptom-guidance + doctor-recommendation flow (user reports chatbot not responding/working as expected).
